title: BLS CPI-U — Boston-Cambridge-Newton, MA-NH (Annual Average) — Massachusetts Inflation Proxy
description: Annual-average Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) for the Boston-Cambridge-Newton, MA-NH metropolitan area, all items, not seasonally adjusted (BLS series CUURS11ASA0, 1982-84=100). Used as the Massachusetts inflation proxy because BLS publishes no statewide Massachusetts CPI. Carries the verbatim BLS annual-average index plus a computed year-over-year inflation rate and the Boston-minus-national rate gap (how much hotter or cooler Boston-area inflation ran than the national CPI-U).

columns.name: year
columns.description: Calendar year of the annual-average index.

columns.name: annual_avg_index
columns.description: BLS annual-average CPI-U index (M13 annual average), Boston-Cambridge-Newton MA-NH, all items, NSA, 1982-84=100. Verbatim BLS.

columns.name: inflation_rate_pct
columns.description: Computed year-over-year percent change of annual_avg_index vs. the prior year. Blank if the prior year is unavailable.

columns.name: boston_minus_national_pct
columns.description: Computed gap: Boston-metro inflation_rate_pct minus the national CPI-U inflation_rate_pct (percentage points). Positive = the Boston area ran hotter than the nation that year.

geographic_coverage: Boston-Cambridge-Newton, MA-NH metropolitan area (BLS proxy for Massachusetts — no statewide MA CPI is published)
update_cadence: Semiannual (BLS publishes Boston-area CPI semiannually; annual averages each January)
notes: No statewide Massachusetts CPI exists; BLS publishes only the Boston-Cambridge-Newton, MA-NH metropolitan series, used here as the MA inflation proxy and explicitly labeled as such in geographic_coverage. The annual_avg_index column is verbatim BLS (M13 annual-average rows from the BLS Public Data API v2 via scripts/pull_bls_cpi.py); inflation_rate_pct and boston_minus_national_pct are computed in-repo. boston_minus_national_pct is the Boston-metro year-over-year rate minus the national CPI-U (CUUR0000SA0) year-over-year rate, in percentage points (positive = Boston ran hotter than the nation). Any year lacking a published annual average is left blank, never interpolated.
